title: Audit logs — how to access and interpret them
content: Audit logs track all configuration changes, logins, and API usage within your CloudDash account for security and compliance purposes. To access audit logs, go to Settings > Security > Audit Logs (Pro and Enterprise plans only). Each log entry contains the timestamp, user email, IP address, action performed (e.g., `dashboard.deleted`, `user.invited`), and the resource ID affected. Logs are retained for 90 days on Pro and 1 year on Enterprise. You can also stream audit logs to an external S3 bucket via the Integrations page. This section provides additional context to ensure comprehensive documentation. The CloudDash platform is built with robustness and scalability in mind. When encountering issues, always verify your configuration settings and ensure that your network allows communication with our endpoints. If you continue to experience difficulties, please consult the troubleshooting steps outlined previously. Our support team is available to assist you with any advanced configurations or unexpected behavior. Remember to keep your agent versions up to date to benefit from the latest features and security patches. Furthermore, monitoring your usage limits can prevent unexpected service interruptions. For detailed API specifications, refer to our developer portal. We are committed to providing reliable service and continuous improvements to our platform. Should you have any feedback or feature requests, do not hesitate to reach out. Thank you for choosing CloudDash as your monitoring solution. We hope this guide has been helpful in resolving your query.
